Abominable (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $60,761,390Details
International Box Office $127,907,499
Worldwide Box Office $188,668,889
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $9,810,672
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $11,337,149
Total $21,147,821
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When teenage Yi encounters a young Yeti on the roof of her apartment building in Shanghai, she and her mischievous friends, Jin and Peng, name him “Everest” and embark on an epic quest to reunite the magical creature with his family at the highest point on Earth. But the trio of friends will have to stay one-step ahead of Burnish, a wealthy man intent on capturing a Yeti, and zoologist Dr. Zara, to help Everest get home.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$20,612,100 (33.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.95 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$75,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,242 opening theaters/4,248 max. theaters, 4.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $79,328,802

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 27th, 2019 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: September 19th, 2019 (Wide) (Uruguay)
September 19th, 2019 (Wide) (New Zealand)
September 20th, 2019 (Wide) (Australia)
September 25th, 2019 (Wide) (Egypt)
September 25th, 2019 (Wide) (Trinidad)
Video Release: December 3rd, 2019 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some action and mild rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2571 (Cert #52124), 3/27/2019)
Running Time: 85 minutes
Film format: 3-D
Geographical setting: China
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Fugitive / On the Run, Non-Chronological, Road Trip, Workaholic, Yeti
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Pearl Studio, DreamWorks Animation
Production Countries: United States, China
Languages: English

Synopsis

When teenage Yi encounters a young Yeti on the roof of her apartment building in Shanghai, she and her mischievous friends, Jin and Peng, name him “Everest” and embark on an epic quest to reunite the magical creature with his family at the highest point on Earth. But the trio of friends will have to stay one-step ahead of Burnish, a wealthy man intent on capturing a Yeti, and zoologist Dr. Zara, to help Everest get home.

Opening Weekend:$20,612,100 (33.9% of total gross)

Domestic Share:32.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)

Theater counts:4,242 opening theaters/4,248 max. theaters, 4.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $79,328,802
